author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
mṛgamada⋅candana, kare kari’ sakhīgaṇa,
śramajala bindu bindu, śobhā kare mukha⋅indu,
adhare muralī kibā bāje

hāsya⋅vilāsa⋅rasa, sarala madhura bhāṣa,
narottama⋅manoratha bharu
duhu̐ka vicitra veśa, kusume racita keśa,
locana⋅mohana līlā karu
en_translation:
Holding musk and sandalwood paste in their hands, the sakhīs serve the Divine Couple. Beads of perspiration adorn Their moon-like faces, As the flute plays so wondrously upon His lips.

With the nectar of playful laughter and simple, sweet speech, May the desires of Narottama’s heart be fulfilled. Both are dressed in exquisite raiment with hair entwined with flowers; May They perform Their eye-enchanting pastimes
